TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version (7.35.20709.0) updated on machine:- DC03, BWH-HQ-WINEDGE, BWH-HQ-SQL-01, BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Connected to the machines (DC03, BWH-HQ-WINEDGE, BWH-HQ-SQL-01, BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01) and discovered they were running an older version of CrowdStrike. Attempted to push the sensor from the portal, but the process was unsuccessful.so connect to the system and uninstalled the older version, downloaded, and installed the latest sensor version (7.35.20709.0) on these machines. Verified the update through the portal. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Manage-Engine Latest version 11.5.2600.32.W updated on BWH-HQ-SQL-01, BWH-HQ-APP-01, BWH-HQ-RDP-01, BWH-HQ-IIS-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed the RMM portal and identified that the BWH-HQ-SQL-01, BWH-HQ-APP-01, BWH-HQ-RDP-01, BWH-HQ-IIS-01 were running an outdated version of ManageEngine. downloaded and installed the latest version (11.5.2600.32.W) of the ManageEngine agent. scan the system with the portal that the device now runs the updated agent version

TimeEntry.Title: MyRMM Manage-Engine Latest version (11.5.2600.32.W) updated:- BWH-HQ-DT-01, BWH-HQ-DT-02, BWH-HQ-LT-07, BWLAP-15
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the portal, Found New Agent Version (11.5.2600.32.W) was released, and Pushed the sensor from the portal but it failed so connected to the below machine(BWH-HQ-DT-01, BWH-HQ-DT-02, BWH-HQ-LT-07, BWLAP-15) and Tried to update the version but got an error (old version is already installed on it) so removed the earlier installed version. Downloaded and installed the new version after verifying the same from the portal, Devices are up to date with the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: MyRemote Version Update for BWH Machines
TimeEntry.Notes:
Connected to multiple BWH workstations and confirmed the MyRemote version update for the following systems:

  1. Machines Checked:
    • BWLAP-06
    • BWHS017-PC
    • BWHS019-PC
    • BWLAP-08
  2. Action Taken:
    • MyRemote client version updated to 26.1.20.9571 on all listed systems.
    • Ensured functionality and proper operation post-update.
    • Confirmed systems are connected and functioning as expected.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: HELP Urgent
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary: Missing Emails and Subfolders Issue

Issue Description:
Received an email from Tracy regarding missing subfolders in her Outlook folders. The folder was present, but the subfolders containing emails were missing.

Actions Taken:

  • Rishad and I connected to the system and searched for the missing folders in both Outlook and the web version, but could not locate them.
  • Ran an audit from the previous center, which revealed that multiple emails had been deleted. However, no related emails were found in the deleted items folder.
  • Despite multiple attempts, we were unable to recover the missing folders or emails.
  • Connected to the VM365 backup server and attempted to retrieve the user's emails from the backup.
  • Successfully retrieved the emails and created a PST file.
  • Shared the PST file with Tracy and connected it to our system to map the PST.
  • Verified that the PST contained the missing emails, and Tracy confirmed that the emails from her previous folders were found.

Outcome:
The missing emails were successfully retrieved, and the issue is resolved. The ticket is now closed.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH Visit Desktop swap for the user
TimeEntry.Notes:
Visited BWH to perform a desktop swap for the user. Set up the workstation and applied the requested display personalization changes, then confirmed with the user that everything was functioning as expected. Checked with PON to verify whether any other users were experiencing issues; none were reported.

The desktop was brought back to the office and fully set up so the user can remote into it if needed.

Mario is currently facing an issue with OneDrive; I guided him to use the Teams channel as an alternative for easier access and advised him to leave his PC powered on when stepping away so we can continue troubleshooting. His PC requires a restart for the changes to fully take effect, which will be verified tomorrow once he returns.

TimeEntry.Title: MyRMM Manage-Engine Latest version (11.5.2600.32.W) updated:-BWH-HQ-VBR-01, BWH-HQ-VONE, BWH-HQ-WINEDGE, DC03, DC04
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the portal, Found New Agent Version (11.5.2600.32.W) was released, and Pushed the sensor from the portal but it failed so connected to the below machine(BWH-HQ-VBR-01, BWH-HQ-VONE, BWH-HQ-WINEDGE, DC03, DC04)and Tried to update the version but got an error (old version is already installed on it) so removed the earlier installed version. Downloaded and installed the new version after verifying the same from the portal, Devices are up to date with the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: MyRMM Manage-Engine Latest version (11.5.2600.32.W) updated:- BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01, RA01, RG01, BWWS-Conf2
TimeEntry.Notes: Manage Engine: Reviewed the RMM portal and identified that the BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01, RA01, RG01,  BWWS-Conf2 were running an outdated version of ManageEngine. downloaded and installed the latest version (11.5.2600.32.W) of the ManageEngine agent. scan the system with the portal that the device now runs the updated agent version

TimeEntry.Title: Traci email
TimeEntry.Notes:
She accidently deleted folder from mailbox, assisted to retrieve the mails. 

Dave assisted with 365 dashboard.

TimeEntry.Title: Virtual Disk consolidation required - BWH-HQ-IIS-01.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server BWH-HQ-IIS-01 is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully and then login to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine.

Login to the vCenter and check the following machines needs to upgrade the VMware tool on it. Go to the VM and the initiate upgrade VMware tool on the machine successfully.

BWH-HQ-RDP-01, BWH-HQ-SQL-01

TimeEntry.Title: MyRMM Manage-Engine Latest version (11.5.2600.32.W) updated:- BWH-HQ-DT-04 and BWH-HQ-LT-05
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the portal, Found New Agent Version (11.5.2600.32.W) was released, and Pushed the sensor from the portal but it failed so connected to the below machine (BWH-HQ-DT-04 and BWH-HQ-LT-05) from Manage Engine, Tried to update the version but got an error (old version is already installed on it) so removed the earlier installed version. Downloaded and installed the new version after verifying the same from the portal, Devices are up to date with the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: myle machine newstar
TimeEntry.Notes: myle issue with newstar. reinstall the application.

TimeEntry.Title: Help to make Newstar work
TimeEntry.Notes: worked with team on the NEWSTAR login and document/checklist access issue. verified the document path, shared folder access, and permissions, all of which were correct. found the workstation was not properly domain joined, so joined it to the domain and completed the required setup, including OneDrive, Outlook, and the user profile. I also reinstalled NEWSTAR and confirmed SQL/application server connectivity. Since the issue still persists, the next step is to raise a support ticket with the vendor.

TimeEntry.Title: Help to make Newstar work
TimeEntry.Notes:
Connected with NewStar support on the system to troubleshoot the issue that had been pending since yesterday. Followed multiple troubleshooting steps including reinstalling drivers and testing different methods, but the issue persisted. As advised by NewStar support, uninstalled and reinstalled both the NewStar application and Microsoft 365 (Office apps).

After reinstallation, verified the EnDrive server connection and tested access to NewStar. Successfully accessed documents, confirming functionality was restored. Contacted the user, explained how to access and use the application moving forward, and confirmed everything was working as expected. Issue resolved and ticket closed.

TimeEntry.Title: Help to make Newstar work
TimeEntry.Notes: Called the Newstar support and connected with on the system to troubleshoot the issue is related to the Microsoft 365 (Office apps). After reinstallation, verified the N-Drive server connection and tested access to NewStar. Successfully accessed documents, confirming functionality was restored. confirmed everything was working as expected. Issue resolved and ticket closed.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH-HQ-LT-09, BWH-HQ-DT-02, BWH-HQ-LT-01 : Device found critical in OPS manager
TimeEntry.Notes: BWH-HQ-DT-02 and BWH-HQ-LT-09 were found down due to incorrect SNMP credentials. The devices were removed and re-added, and they are now up. BWH-HQ-LT-01 was found to be misconfigured, so it was also re-added. All devices are now up in Ops Manager, and there are no active alarms.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Restoring ESXI 10.1.1.10 and 10.1.1.14 and Veeam backup.
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary: ESXi Host Password Sync Issue & Veeam Backup Failures – BWH Client

Worked on an issue for the BWH client involving two ESXi hosts where password synchronization had failed, resulting in account lockouts and subsequent Veeam backup failures across all configured jobs.

Collaborated with Sai, Rishad, and Gurdeep, with onsite support provided to assist in troubleshooting. Initial steps included attempting to reboot the affected hosts; however, the password replication issue persisted, preventing successful authentication.

To proceed without impacting the existing datastore or VM data, accessed the hosts via console and mounted an ESXi ISO. Performed a password reset using recovery methods, ensuring there was no disruption to the datastore or hosted virtual machines. After successfully resetting and reconfiguring the credentials, access to the hosts was restored.

Post-reset, it was observed that the account continued to get locked. Further investigation identified that Veeam backup services were repeatedly attempting authentication using outdated credentials, which caused continuous lockouts.

To resolve the issue:

  • Removed all existing Veeam backup jobs and host configurations associated with the old credentials
  • Cleared stored credentials within Veeam
  • Re-added the ESXi hosts into Veeam using updated credentials
  • Reconfigured backup jobs and validated connectivity

After completing the reconfiguration, services were restarted and verification was performed to ensure:

  • No further account lockouts were occurring
  • Veeam backups were functioning successfully
  • All hosts were stable and accessible

Final Status: Issue fully resolved. ESXi hosts are accessible with updated credentials, and Veeam backups are running successfully without errors.

TimeEntry.Title: Restoring ESXI 10.1.1.10 and 10.1.1.14 and Veeam backup.
TimeEntry.Notes: Backup jobs for BWH were failing, following which Sai and Rishad visited the site and initiated troubleshooting involving two ESXi hosts. The issue was identified as a password synchronization failure, which caused repeated account lockouts and resulted in Veeam backup failures across all configured jobs. Troubleshooting was carried out collaboratively with Sai, Rishad, and Praveen, with onsite support provided. Initial remediation included rebooting the affected hosts; however, the password replication issue persisted and authentication continued to fail. To resolve the issue without impacting the existing datastore or virtual machines, the hosts were accessed via console and an ESXi ISO was mounted to perform a password reset using recovery methods, ensuring no disruption to datastore or VM data. After resetting and reconfiguring the credentials, access to the hosts was restored; however, subsequent monitoring showed continued account lockouts. Further investigation revealed that Veeam backup services were repeatedly attempting authentication using outdated credentials, causing the lockouts. To remediate this, all existing Veeam backup jobs and host configurations associated with the old credentials were removed, stored credentials in Veeam were cleared, the ESXi hosts were re-added using updated credentials, and backup jobs were reconfigured and validated. Services were restarted, and verification confirmed that account lockouts had ceased, ESXi hosts were stable and accessible, and Veeam backups were functioning successfully. A test run of backup jobs was performed; a few jobs initially failed and were subsequently retried with full backups, after which the backups completed successfully. The issue is now fully resolved.

TimeEntry.Title: Manage-Engine Latest version updated 11.5.2600.32.W -BWH-HQ-LT-26, BWWS016-PC, BWWS017-PC, BWWS029-PC
TimeEntry.Notes: Review the My RMM portal, and found the machine BWH-HQ-LT-26, BWWS016-PC, BWWS017-PC, BWWS029-PC running with old version of Manage-Engine. So uninstalled the OLD version and downloaded and installed the latest version (11.5.2600.32.W) of the manage-engine agent. Verified from the portal, device is running with updated agent version.

TimeEntry.Title: Missing and failed patch update on the systems:- BWH-HQ-LT-01, BWWS-CONF2, BWH-HQ-LT-13, BWH-HQ-LT-26, BWH-HQ-CONF-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login to the manage engine portal and check approved patches for the below mentioned machine then download and installed. connected to the machine through MY RMM and installed the failed patches manually which are applicable to the computer. Then reboot the machine and scan the machine for installed patches. After scan Check and verify the machine with the MY RMM portal. The failed patches are updated successfully.

Machine is: - BWH-HQ-LT-01, BWWS-CONF2, BWH-HQ-LT-13, BWH-HQ-LT-26, BWH-HQ-CONF-01

Patch installed: -1319983 Meteor Lake-P [Intel Arc Graphics][PCI\\VEN_8086&DEV_7D55] (32.0.101.8424)

1319982 Meteor Lake-P[PCI\\VEN_8086&DEV_7D45] (32.0.101.8424)

1319959 Wireless-AC 9560 [Jefferson Peak][PCI\\VEN_8086&DEV_A370] (24.20.0.4)

1319955 Meteor Lake PCH CNVi WiFi[PCI\\VEN_8086&DEV_7E40] (24.20.0.4)

1319954 Wireless-AC 9560[PCI\\VEN_8086&DEV_7AF0] (24.20.0.4)

1319951 Raptor Lake PCH CNVi WiFi[PCI\\VEN_8086&DEV_51F1] (24.20.0.4)

1319948 Wi-Fi 7(802.11be) AX1775*/AX1790*/BE20*/BE401/BE1750* 2x2[PCI\\VEN_8086&DEV_272B] (24.20.0.4)

357629 Zoom Workplace (EXE) (x64) (User Based) (7.0.1.34140)

 1319978 Comet Lake PCH CNVi WiFi[PCI\\VEN_8086&DEV_06F0] (24.20.2.1)

TimeEntry.Title: BWH-HQ-LT-01,BWWS-Conf2,BWH-HQ-LT-09,BWH-HQ-LT-08,BWH-HQ-LT-07 has Missing patch while last run

I worked on the missing patches and logged into the ManageEngine portal to review the approved patches for the machines. I downloaded and manually installed the applicable failed patches.then rebooted..Scanned the devices Some patches were not applicable due to compatibility issues. All previously missing patches were successfully updated.


Patches are 
Raptor Lake PCH CNVi WiFi[PCI\\VEN_8086&DEV_51F1] (24.20.0.4)

Machines are :BWH-HQ-LT-01,BWWS-Conf2,BWH-HQ-LT-09,BWH-HQ-LT-08,BWH-HQ-LT-07

TimeEntry.Title: BWLAP-10 : Critical low disk space alert found on this machine
TimeEntry.Notes: I accessed the machine to analyze disk space usage and identify areas of high consumption. By following the standard disk cleanup procedures, including removing temporary files, system cache, and unnecessary application data, I was able to free up approximately 5 GB of disk space, improving overall system performance.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: BWH-HQ-LT-07,BWH-HQ-LT-13,BWH-HQ-LT-26,BWH-HQ-DT-01,TABLET-AB7MOH3D : Manually patch update as APD failed to execute
TimeEntry.Notes:
• BWH-HQ-LT-07
    • Meteor Lake-P driver [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_7D45] (32.0.101.8424) updated.
    • Meteor Lake PCH CNVi WiFi driver [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_7E40] (24.20.0.4) updated.
  • BWH-HQ-LT-13
    • Meteor Lake-P Intel Arc Graphics driver [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_7D55] (32.0.101.8424) updated.
    • Wi-Fi 7 (802.11be) AX1775*/AX1790*/BE20*/BE401/BE1750* 2x2 driver [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_272B] (24.20.0.4) updated.
  • BWH-HQ-LT-26
    • Raptor Lake PCH CNVi WiFi driver [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_51F1] (24.20.0.4) updated.
  • BWH-HQ-DT-01
    • Wireless-AC 9560 Jefferson Peak driver [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_A370] (24.20.0.4) updated.
  • TABLET-AB7MOH3D
    • Microsoft ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server (x64) (17.10.6.1) installed.
    • Microsoft OLE DB Driver 18 for SQL Server (x64) (18.7.5.0) installed.
    • 2026-01 Cumulative Update Preview for .NET Framework 3.5 and 4.8.1 for Windows 11, version 25H2 (KB5074828) applied.
    • TigerLake-LP GT2 Intel Iris Xe Graphics driver [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_9A49] (32.0.101.7079) updated.
  • Verified that all the above updates are reflected in the MyRMM database.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Performed Weekly Firewall Backup for BWH:- Sophos firewall
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the BWH:- Sophos firewall. Downloaded the backup file and placed the same file on the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Cisco Secure Client(Version:5.1.16.194) Manual Update Required on BWH-HQ-VBR-01,BWH-HQ-SQL-01,BWH-HQ-RDP-01,BWH-HQ-IIS-01
TimeEntry.Notes: After reviewing the management portal, it has been identified that the following systems require manual updates:BWH-HQ-VBR-01,BWH-HQ-SQL-01,BWH-HQ-RDP-01,BWH-HQ-IIS-01. The following components need to be manually uploaded and installed:Cisco Secure Client & AnyConnect VPN (5.1.16.194) Manual installation required Cisco Secure Client Diagnostics and Reporting Tool (5.1.16.194)  Manual installation required Remote access will be used to download and install the required setup files on the affected systems. Once the installation is completed, a scan will be initiated through the management portal to verify that the systems reflect the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Joanne laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: Taking backup from the machine. before adding the ssd on the slot

TimeEntry.Title: New Star login issue for User Nancy Hayden
TimeEntry.Notes: Received the call on support from Nancy Hayden for having login issue with New Star. So connected to user machine BWLAP-10. Checked VPN settings. VPN was connected, but still user was not able to access any remote app. So checked RemoteApp and Desktop Connections settings in control panel. It was showing connection failed. Tried to update it from control panel, but unable to update. Tried to open the remote apps on web, but same error. So disconnect the VPN, reboot machine. After reboot connected the VPN again, and update the RemoteApp and Desktop Connections settings in control panel. It is updated successfully now. And user is able to connect the New Star now.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH-HQ-DT-05 : All security tools installation
TimeEntry.Notes: Given system is completed by me with all security tools ( screenconnect,manage engine, crowdstrike,huntress and cisco-umbrella) with latest version of that. Also configured hostname and power options  as per the policy.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Your veeam license having issue on BWH-HQ-VONE
TimeEntry.Notes: During the Veeam backup check, I noticed that the VSPC portal was showing a warning that the Veeam license had issues. To resolve this, I connected to the BWH-HQ-VONE server and verified that the license was running in the grace period. I then logged into the Veeam Pulse portal, , downloaded the required license file, and initiated the installation process. Once the installation was completed, I restarted the VSPC agent service on the server and verified that the updated license is now visible on the VSPC portal.

TimeEntry.Title: Read.AI
TimeEntry.Notes:
"

Hello,

It looks like there is a read.ai meeting notes requested or started by me but I am not sure what that is how do I take it off? I don’t believe I have a log in so I just want to make sure this is added by Brandywine?" - User has concern about read.ai. Issue has beem solved. Called the user and connected with her in remote desktop and explained and the issue is resolved found out read.ai was not added by the user in the teams it might be added by the user outside the organization.

TimeEntry.Title: Joanne laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: installed the second SSD on the machine. Installed windows 11 and the tools.

Downloaded and installed the new version after verifying the same from the portal, Devices are up to date with the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version (7.35.20709.0) updated on machine:- BWH-HQ-CONF-01, BWH-HQ-DT-01, BWH-HQ-DT-02, BWH-HQ-DT-03, BWH-HQ-DT-05
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines BWH-HQ-CONF-01, BWH-HQ-DT-01, BWH-HQ-DT-02, BWH-HQ-DT-03, BWH-HQ-DT-05 running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.35.20709.0 of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Virtual Disk consolidation required - BWH-HQ-VONE.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server BWH-HQ-VONE is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully Then logged in to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine

TimeEntry.Title: Update missing patch on the BWH-HQ-LT-10
TimeEntry.Notes:
Device BWH-HQ-LT-10 was identified as non-compliant due to missing patches.

Logged in to the ManageEngine portal and verified pending updates on the system. Proceeded with patch remediation by installing the following updates:

  • Intel Meteor Lake-P Graphics Driver – Version 32.0.101.8424 (PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_7D45)
  • Intel Meteor Lake PCH CNVi WiFi Driver – Version 24.20.0.4 (PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_7E40)
  • Zoom Workplace (EXE) (x64) (User Based) – Version 7.0.2.34412
  • Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise (Current Channel, x64) – Version 2603 (Build 19822.20142)
  • 2026-03 Cumulative Update for Windows 11, Version 25H2 for x64-based Systems – KB5086672 (OS Build 26200.8117)

After installation, performed a manual patch scan to verify deployment status. The updates were successfully installed; however, the system is currently pending restart to complete the update process.

User confirmation is required to restart the device to finalize the installation and clear the pending patch status.

TimeEntry.Title: Manual Update Required: Cisco Secure Client v5.1.16.194 on BWLAP-15,BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01,Loaner-03
TimeEntry.Notes:
After reviewing the management portal, it has been identified that the following systems require manual updates:

BWLAP-15
BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01
Loaner-03

Cisco Secure Client & AnyConnect VPN (Version 5.1.16.194) requires manual installation on these devices. Remote access will be utilized to download and install the necessary setup files on the affected systems.
Once the installation is complete, a scan will be initiated through the management portal to verify that the systems are updated to the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Observed a synchronization issue in Manage Engine on the following machines:BWLAP-06,BWH-HQ-DT-04, BWWS019-PC
TimeEntry.Notes:
Logged into the Manage Engine portal.
Checked the agent status for the listed machines.BWLAP-06,BWH-HQ-DT-04, BWWS019-PC
Identified synchronization issues.
Performed necessary troubleshooting steps to re-establish communication between agents and the Manage Engine portal.
Outcome:

Synchronization issue has been resolved.
All machines are now syncing properly with the Manage Engine server.
Systems are working as expected.

TimeEntry.Title: Cisco Umbrella Agent Syncing Issue with Portal for machines BWH-HQ-APP-01,BWH-HQ-DT-01,RA01,RG01
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the Cisco Umbrella portal and identified that machines BWH-HQ-APP-01,BWH-HQ-DT-01,RA01,RG01 were experiencing syncing issues with the Cisco Umbrella portal. I then connected to the affected machines and addressed the network connectivity settings. After ensuring proper communication between the agent and the portal, the agent is now successfully syncing and communicating with the Cisco Umbrella cloud, with the correct status and configuration reflected in the portal.

TimeEntry.Title: DESKTOP Alert - Critical - BWWS029-PC.brandywinedev.localMemory Utilization
TimeEntry.Notes:
Worked on the assigned ticket. Logged into the OPS manager and observed machine BWWS029 has memory threshold error. So
Connected to machine from manage engine and killed the unnecessary task from task manager. After completing these steps,
 all sensors resumed normal operation. As a result, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: apps.brandywine-homes.com getting 404 error.
TimeEntry.Notes: Worked with Parveen on the remote URL for the BWH and verified its not working after connecting to the VPN, Verified on the local server it was not working so rebooted both RA-RG servers and troubleshooted it and verified it working on local machines but on the RDP its having the issues.

TimeEntry.Title: Joanne laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: newstar application - installation using VPN.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH-Joanne system Set-up for user joanne pagliarulo
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per the Handover by Rishad, on New system.

Installed BlueBeam and Adobe Reader to the system..

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version (7.35.20709.0) updated on machine:- BWH-HQ-CONF-01, BWH-HQ-DT-02, BWH-HQ-LT-07, BWH-HQ-LT-10
TimeEntry.Notes: Connected to the machines (BWH-HQ-CONF-01, BWH-HQ-DT-02, BWH-HQ-LT-07, BWH-HQ-LT-10) and discovered they were running an older version of CrowdStrike. Attempted to push the sensor from the portal, but the process was unsuccessful.so connect to the system and uninstalled the older version, downloaded, and installed the latest sensor version (7.35.20709.0) on these machines. Verified the update through the portal. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Update Missing patch on the BWLAP-19
TimeEntry.Notes:
Device BWLAP-19 was identified as non-compliant due to missing patches.

Logged in to the ManageEngine portal and verified pending updates on the system. Proceeded with patch remediation by installing the following updates:

  • Intel Meteor Lake-P Graphics Driver – Version 32.0.101.8424 (PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_7D45)
  • Intel Meteor Lake PCH CNVi WiFi Driver – Version 24.20.0.4 (PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_7E40)
  • Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise (Current Channel, x64) – Version 2603 (Build 19822.20142)
  • 2026-03 Cumulative Update for Windows 11, Version 25H2 for x64-based Systems – KB5086672 (OS Build 26200.8117)

After installation, performed a manual patch scan to verify successful deployment and to clear the non-compliant status.

Post validation, confirmed that all patches have been successfully applied and no high vulnerabilities remain. The system is now compliant as per patch management policy.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Ticket #: 202-00-592442 Assigned -- NSE | Newstar Loggin issue on remote server
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the handover, investigated the login issue on the remote server with the support team. A case has been opened with the support team, and the update has been shared with the lead for further investigation.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Ticket #: 202-00-592442 Assigned -- NSE | Newstar Loggin issue on remote server
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the RDP server and user machine to test the Newstar to check the issues. Troubleshoot the issue on user machine and see there is some issue on application side. Open a case with support and then setup the webex session with support team. After that start working with them on remote session. After that found another issue as well which was related to network drive with user. Logged into RA01 server with user credentials directly. After that setup the network drive and verify newstart access for user. It worked fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Virtual Disk consolidation required - BWH-HQ-RDP-01.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server BWH-HQ-RDP-01 is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully and then login to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Daisy tong laptop
TimeEntry.Notes:
Migrate jhoffman files, 

create account for daisy

setup applciations newstar

Newstar sever accesse for constallation, requested by Traci.

TimeEntry.Title: Setup for BWH-HQ-LT-02 - Installation and Configuration for Daisy Tong
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received a request from Rishad for setting up the machine BWH-HQ-LT-02 for Daisy Tong. The following steps were taken to complete the setup:

  1. Data Transfer: Moved all data from Jhomffman to an external hard drive to ensure a clean setup for Daisy.
  2. Login: Logged into the machine with Daisy's credentials.
  3. Technician Tools Installation: Installed all required Technijian tools on the machine.
  4. NewStart Installation: Installed the NewStart software.
  5. XML Ribbon Installation: Installed the necessary XML Ribbon for Daisy’s work.
  6. Reboots: Rebooted the system twice to ensure all updates and installations were applied correctly.
  7. Also added work resources through url and send the shortcut to desktop
  8. As per request from Rishad Removed jhoffman profile from users .
  9. Sophos VPN: Installed Sophos VPN to enable secure network access.
  10. Unistalled Forticlient

The Outlook and Teams configurations are pending because the necessary credentials were not available at the time of setup.

Updated Rishad on the progress and mentioned that I am waiting for the credentials to complete the final setup for Outlook and Teams.

Ticket will remain open until the missing credentials are provided for completing the setup.

TimeEntry.Title: New Sharefile Users for Glendora Mountain View Crossing
TimeEntry.Notes: Received the email on support from Saule for having issue with not receiving the emails from mail@sf-notifications.com. Checked the user's machine as emails were not being delivered to their mailbox. Logged into the O365 Admin Console and traced the emails, which were found to be quarantined. Added the sender to the allow list and released the emails from quarantine. After these actions, the user was able to successfully reset their password, and ShareFile login is now working as expected.

TimeEntry.Title: NewStar application installation on machine BWH-HQ-DT-05
TimeEntry.Notes: As per assigned task by Rishad, installed the NewStar application on machine BWH-HQ-DT-05. Also installed informXL. After installation updated the same to Rishad.

TimeEntry.Title: apps.brandywine-homes.com getting 404 error.
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

Issue:
User is encountering a 404 error while accessing the website apps.brandywine-homes.com.

Actions Taken:

  1. Connected to the server and user machine to replicate the issue.
  2. Attempted to resolve the issue on the gateway server.
  3. Investigated and made changes on the application server.
  4. Verified all relevant settings on the user's system, but the issue persisted.

Current Status:
The issue remains unresolved. A support ticket has been opened, and we are currently waiting for the support team to address and resolve the issue.

Next Steps:
Awaiting resolution from support.

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version V0.14.150 on BWH-HQ-DT-03, BWH-HQ-LT-02, BWWS022-PC, BWWS027-PC
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into the Huntress portal, we verified that the Huntress Agent Version on the following servers on is old version V0.14.146. Connected to the machines (WH-HQ-DT-03, BWH-HQ-LT-02, BWWS022-PC, BWWS027-PC) from manage engine, download and installed the latest version V0.14.150. After that verified in the portal it is updated to latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Screenconnect Latest version (v 26.1.25.9592) update: - BW-ACCT02, BWH-HQ-DT-03, BWH-HQ-LT-01, BWH-HQ-LT-08
TimeEntry.Notes: Checked and found that the devices (BW-ACCT02, BWH-HQ-DT-03, BWH-HQ-LT-01, BWH-HQ-LT-08) were running an outdated version of Screen Connect. I initiated the update for these devices from the portal, but unable to update, I noticed some issues when verifying the version on the portal. connected to the machines through My Rmm and uninstall the screen connect software. after that download and install the fresh My Remote setup and install on it and verify that agents have been updated successfully with the latest version (v 26.1.25.9592) Hence closing the ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: "BWH-HQ-LT-10 Version Update to 26.1.25.9592"
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received a request to update the version for BWH-HQ-LT-10. Logged into the system and confirmed that the version 26.1.25.9592 was successfully updated.

Performed the following actions:

  1. Verified the current version.
  2. Downloaded and installed the latest version (26.1.25.9592).
  3. Restarted the system after installation.
  4. Confirmed that the system is now running the updated version without any issues.

Everything seems to be working fine post-update. Please let me know if you face any issues. The ticket will remain open for any further support.

Ticket closed after confirmation from the user.

TimeEntry.Title: Desktop swap and Laptop delivery
TimeEntry.Notes: Handed over Laptop to Dave and Desktop to user along with that checked with mario about his one drive past issue.

TimeEntry.Title: apps.brandywine-homes.com getting 404 error.
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:
Subject: Issue Resolution for Newstar Application – DocuSign Access Error

Description:

  • Issue: The apps.brandywine-homes.com URL was showing a 404 error, which was previously resolved. However, when accessing the Newstar application through Sales Live from RD web, the issue persisted when navigating to Deal Lookup and attempting to open DocuSign files. The error message indicated that the file was invalid and could not be found.
  • Resolution Steps:
    1. Tried multiple solutions to resolve the issue internally.
    2. Contacted support as a ticket had already been created by the previous shift.
    3. During the call with support, identified that the issue was related to permissions and mapping.
    4. After adjusting the permissions and verifying with the user, the issue was resolved.
    5. The Newstar application now functions correctly both locally and through the RDM web.

Status: Resolved
Action Taken: Permissions and mapping issue fixed, verified with the user.

TimeEntry.Title: ND-BWH monthly network scan
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary: Monthly ND Scan and Report Generation

Issue:
As part of the monthly task, logged into the ND portal to run the monthly ND scan and generate the VWS monthly report.

Steps Taken:

  1. Logged into the ND portal as per the monthly task requirements.
  2. Ran the monthly ND scan.
  3. Generated the VWS monthly ND report.
  4. Uploaded and shared the report in the team's channel for reference.

Resolution:
The monthly ND scan was successfully completed, and the report was generated and uploaded to the team's channel as per the task requirements. Task was completed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: Snapshot consolidation required - BWH-HQ-SQL-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server ( BWH-HQ-SQL-01) is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully Then logged in to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine

Then reboot the machine and scan the machine for installed patches. After scan Check and verify the machine with the MY RMM portal. The failed patches are updated successfully.

Machine is: - BWH-HQ-LT-01, BWH-HQ-DT-05, BWWS029-PC

Patch installed: - 357880 Mozilla Firefox (x64) (149.0.2

1319736 CoffeeLake-S GT2 [UHD Graphics P630][PCI\\VEN_8086&DEV_3E96] (31.0.101.2137)

357880 Mozilla Firefox (x64) (149.0.2)

I worked on the missing patches and logged into the ManageEngine portal to review the approved patches for the machines. I downloaded and manually installed the applicable failed patches.then rebooted.Scanned the devices Some patches were not applicable due to compatibility issues. All previously missing patches were successfully updated.

TimeEntry.Notes:
Manually accessed the machines and downloaded all below patches, then deployed them successfully.

BWH-HQ-LT-02:
Installed Microsoft ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server (x64) version 17.10.6.1.
Installed update for Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise (Current Channel, x86) to version 2603 (Build 19822.20168).
Installed Microsoft OLE DB Driver 18 for SQL Server (x64) version 18.7.5.0.
Installed Microsoft Visual C++ 2015–2022 Redistributable (x86) version 14.50.35719.0.
Installed Microsoft Visual C++ 2015–2022 Redistributable (x64) version 14.50.35719.0.
Installed 2026-01 Cumulative Update Preview for .NET Framework 3.5 and 4.8.1 (KB5074828).
Installed 2026-03 Cumulative Update for Windows 11 version 25H2 (KB5086672) (Build 26200.8117).

BWH-HQ-LT-20:
Installed Microsoft OLE DB Driver 18 for SQL Server (x64) version 18.7.5.0.
Installed 2026-03 Cumulative Update for Windows 11 version 25H2 (KB5086672) (Build 26200.8117).
Updated Google Chrome (x64) to versions 147.0.7727.55 / 147.0.7727.56.
Updated Intel Iris Plus Graphics G7 driver to version 31.0.101.2137.
Updated Intel Ice Lake-LP PCH CNVi WiFi driver to version 24.20.2.1.

Verified that all above patches are successfully updated and reflected in the MyRMM database.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

Then reboot the machine and scan the machine for installed patches. After scan Check and verify the machine with the MY RMM portal. The failed patches are updated successfully. TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into the Huntress portal, we verified that the Huntress Agent Version on the following machines on  is old version V0.14.150. Connected to the machines 
DC04,BWH-HQ-SQL-01,Persimmon,BWH-HQ-VONE from manage engine download and installed the latest version Ver0.14.162. After that verified in the portal it is updated to latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: BWLAP-06 : Patch failed due to low disk space, need cleanup
TimeEntry.Notes: Manually accessed the system and carried out a comprehensive disk cleanup to increase available storage space. This included deleting temporary files, clearing cache data, and removing unnecessary system and user-generated files. Unused and obsolete data was also identified and safely removed to optimize storage utilization. As a result, around 15 GB of disk space was reclaimed, leading to improved system performance and stability.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: BWLAP-06,BWH-HQ-LT-20 : Manually patch update, which APD skipped
TimeEntry.Notes:
• Accessed the machines and downloaded the patches with the given IDs, then deployed them successfully on the target systems (BWLAP-06, BWH-HQ-LT-20)

  • 2026-03 Cumulative Update for Windows 11 Version 25H2 (KB5086672, OS Build 26200.8117) – Installed successfully on BWLAP-06

  • Update for Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise (Current Channel), Version 2603 (Build 19822.20168) – Installed successfully on BWH-HQ-LT-20

  • After deploying all patches, a rescan was performed in the RMM tool, and the database was successfully updated with the installed patches.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

Alisal Sales Report Issue: Nancy Heyden

Recovered.

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version V0.14.162 on BWH-HQ-LT-05,BWH-HQ-APP-01,RA01,BWH-HQ-RDP-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into the Huntress portal, we verified that the Huntress Agent Version on the following machines on  is old version V0.14.150. Connected to the machines from manage engine
BWH-HQ-LT-05,BWH-HQ-APP-01,RA01,BWH-HQ-RDP-01 from manage engine download and installed the latest version Ver0.14.162. After that verified in the portal it is updated to latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: SERVER Alert - Critical - BWH-HQ-VONE: (10.1.1.19) - Disk Utilization is 66%, threshold value for this monitor is 65%
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the alert connected to BWH-HQ-VONE, download and install TreeSize, a disk space management tool that provides a detailed overview of the disk usage across folders and files on the system. After installation, TreeSize was run to analyse the disk. TreeSize scans the system and generates a report showing the size of all folders and files in a hierarchical structure so carefully review all folders and files occupying excessive space. Based on the review, we proceeded to clear unnecessary files that were identified as no longer needed. This included: Temporary Files: Files in the system's temporary folder are often created by applications during installation, updates, or other processes; clear these temporary files to free up space. Recycle Bin: emptied the Recycle Bin, where deleted files are temporarily stored. This ensured that deleted files were permanently removed from the disk and no longer took up space. Downloaded Items: System error memory dump files, Old Windows updates, previous Windows installations other system cache files. 42% of the space is free. Now Sufficient Free Space Available.

TimeEntry.Title: QNAP firmware 5.2.9.3451 upgrade for BWH
TimeEntry.Notes: I logged into the QNAP system in the BWH environment and found that firmware updates were pending. Before proceeding with the update, I verified that all backup jobs had completed successfully. Since a reboot was required prior to installing the firmware, I rebooted the QNAP device, installed the updates, and then performed a second reboot to finalise the installation. After the system came back online, I verified that all shared folders were accessible. I then re-scanned the backup repository, and the scan completed successfully.

TimeEntry.Title: BWLAP-10 : New star app not able to connect
TimeEntry.Notes: While accessing the system, it was found that RDP was unable to connect when trying to access Newstar. Further checks showed that the services on RA01 and RG01 were down. After restarting the services, the connection was restored, and the Newstar application is now accessible.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Notes: Rosa had issues files sycning to onedrive, where few folders were not uploading to onedrive web.

Connected to the machines (BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01, DC03, BWH-HQ-VBR-01, BWH-HQ-DT-01, BWH-HQ-LT-26,BWWS029-PC) from manage engine, download and installed the latest version V0.14.162. TimeEntry.Title: Manage Engine Synchronization Issue for BWH Machine BWH-HQ-DT-02,BWH-HQ-LT-09 and BWH-HQ-LT-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into Manage engine portal and found the agent on machines BWH-HQ-DT-02,BWH-HQ-LT-09 and BWH-HQ-LT-01 not communication with portal. So connected to the machine, tried to restart the service, but nothing happened. So, Uninstall the Corrupt manage engine then download and the latest manage engine agent. After that agent on machine is communicating with the portal and working fine hence closing this ticket

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version 0.14.162 on BWWS019-PC,BWH-HQ-LT-08,BWLAP-15,BWH-HQ-LT-09
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into the Huntress portal, we verified that the Huntress Agent Version on the following machines on is old version V0.14.150 Connected to the machines BWWS019-PC,BWH-HQ-LT-08,BWLAP-15,BWH-HQ-LT-09 from manage engine, download and installed the latest version V0.14.162 After that verified in the portal it is updated to latest version

TimeEntry.Title: Manage engine agent not syncing with portal on machines BWH-HQ-APP-01,BWH-HQ-IIS-01,BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01,BWH-HQ-RDP-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into Manage engine portal and found the agent on machines BWH-HQ-APP-01,BWH-HQ-IIS-01,BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-01,BWH-HQ-RDP-01
 not communicating with portal. So connected to the machine, tried to restart the service, but nothing happened.
So run the agent troubleshooting tool on machines. After that agent on machine is communicating with the portal.

TimeEntry.Title: Rosa- onedrive
TimeEntry.Notes: noticed if files were missing and shortcuts were not opening. Assigned new path for those files.

TimeEntry.Title: laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: Adobe had an error and reinstalled adobe acrobat on the machine.

TimeEntry.Title: Manage Engine Synchronization Issue for BWH Machine BWH-HQ-LT-13 and BWH-HQ-LT-26
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into Manage engine portal and found the agent on machines BWH-HQ-LT-13 and BWH-HQ-LT-26 not communication with portal. So connected to the machine, tried to restart the service, but nothing happened. So, Uninstall the Corrupt manage engine then download and the latest manage engine agent. After that agent on machine is communicating with the portal and working fine hence closing this ticket

TimeEntry.Title: My Remote stuck on the BWH-HQ-APP-01
TimeEntry.Notes: login to my remote and checked the screen was freezing while trying to log in to the server. Log in to the server through myrmm and check the screenconnect service. restart the services and check again. Now it is working fine hence closing this ticket

TimeEntry.Title: URGENT! need you on this call
TimeEntry.Notes: As per email received from Tracy to join the team meeting and given the access to NewStar support Team. So joined the meeting and work with their support. After this issue is fixed, as confirmed by Traci.

TimeEntry.Title: BWH laptop pickup
TimeEntry.Notes: Did drop the laptop to BWH and informed joseph regarding this.

TimeEntry.Title: URGENT! need you on this call
TimeEntry.Notes: Worked with Satish, who was coordinating with Traci and the Newstar support team, to troubleshoot the issue encountered while generating the report. The problem has now been successfully resolved. Closing the ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: CrowdStrike Sensor Updated with Version 7.36.20805.0: BWH-HQ-APP-01, BWH-HQ-IIS-01, BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-0, BWH-HQ-RDP-01, BWH-HQ-SQL-01, BWH-HQ-VBR-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines BWH-HQ-APP-01, BWH-HQ-IIS-01, BWH-HQ-OPSPRB-0, BWH-HQ-RDP-01, BWH-HQ-SQL-01, BWH-HQ-VBR-01 running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.36.20805.0 of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Joannne printer
TimeEntry.Notes: set up printer to print cheque.

TimeEntry.Title: newstar meeting
TimeEntry.Notes: newstar meeting, set up SQL.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version (7.36.20805.0) updated on machine:- BWH-HQ-CONF-01, BWH-HQ-LT-13, BWWS016-PC, BWWS-CONF2
TimeEntry.Notes: Connected to the machines through my rmm (BWH-HQ-CONF-01, BWH-HQ-LT-13, BWWS016-PC, BWWS-CONF2) and discovered they were running an older version of CrowdStrike. Attempted to push the sensor from the portal, but the process was unsuccessful.so connect to the system and uninstalled the older version, downloaded, and installed the latest sensor version (7.36.20805.0) on these machines. Verified the update through the portal. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Quarterly Phishing Simulation Campaign
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into Phishing tools and then verify the user have be synced with portal. And all the users are able to receive the training emails.

TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.36.20805.0)updated on machines : BWH-HQ-DT-03,BWH-HQ-LT-17 and BWH-HQ-LT-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machinesBWH-HQ-DT-03,BWH-HQ-LT-17 and BWH-HQ-LT-01 running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version (7.36.20805.0) of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Inform XL issue and network printer
TimeEntry.Notes:
• Accessed the machine BWH-HQ-LT-15 and set up both network printers (ANNEX and CORP).
  • Verified successful printing with the user.
  • Diagnosed the InformXL issue and reinstalled the application.
  • Reconfigured all necessary settings and confirmed the ribbon was visible in Excel.
  • Data was not being fetched from the server; the issue was further resolved by Pravin.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Inform XL issue and network printer
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:
Worked with Suresh to resolve issues on the user’s machine related to the installation of InformXL. Connected to the system and performed a reboot as it had been running for an extended period. Post-reboot, verified all required services for the installation.

Identified that while the services were installed, InformXL was not functioning correctly. Reviewed the initial configuration settings and proceeded with a reinstallation of InformXL. After setup, verified the SQL Server native connection and conducted testing, which confirmed normal functionality.

Issue resolved and ticket closed.
